data.title: JPMorgan Chase GAAP EPS of $5.94 beats by $0.48, revenue of $50.5B beats by $1.59B
data.description:
• JPMorgan Chase press release (JPM): Q1 GAAP EPS of $5.94 beats by $0.48.
  • Revenue of $50.5B (+9.8% Y/Y) beats by $1.59B.
  • Net revenue was $50.5 billion, up 10%. Net interest income was $25.5 billion, up 9%. Noninterest revenue was $25.1 billion, up 11%.
  • The provision for credit losses was $2.5 billion. Net charge-offs were $2.3 billion, down $16 million. The net reserve build was $191 million, and included a $327 million net build in Wholesale and a $139 million net release in Consumer. In the prior year, the provision was $3.3 billion, net charge-offs were $2.3 billion and the net reserve build was $973 million.
  • Shares +1.4% PM.

data.title: BTIG favors Mondelez, Smucker, and Utz as it launches food sector coverage
data.description:
BTIG initiated coverage on the food sector with a heavy mix of Neutral ratings due to the challenging backdrop. 

Analyst Rob Dickerson highlighted that sector multiples are discounted on a historic basis for many companies in the sector because of sector demand, cost, and leverage concerns. Looking ahead, Dickerson and his team think valuations will stay depressed without improved core business demand, revenue growth, boosted margins, and capital structures.

Food stocks that BTIG started off coverage with a Neutral rating included BellRing Brands (BRBR), Campbell Soup (CPB), Conagra Brands (CAG), General Mills (GIS), Hershey (HSY), Kraft Heinz (KHC), McCormick (MKC), Post Holdings (POST), and Simply Good Foods (SMPL).

BTIG did find some three food stocks to recommend on what the firm views as safer, more defensive, cost-tailwind, cash step-up names amid an otherwise challenging operating backdrop.

Mondelez (MDLZ) is rated Buy because BTIG sees best-in-class global positioning and expects cocoa deflation from 2027 to drive significant margin expansion, above-algorithm EPS growth, and a strong low–double-digit free cash flow CAGR, leaving the current 17x forward P/E as overly discounted versus its growth and quality. J.M. Smucker (SJM) also landed a Buy rating as BTIG believes coffee profit expansion from lower arabica prices and tariff add-backs is underappreciated, supporting 20%+ segment EBIT growth next year and earnings above its high-single-digit algorithm and consensus at an attractive ~8x forward EBITDA. Meanwhile, Utz Brands (UTZ) is rated Buy because BTIG sees differentiated sales, profit, and free cash flow potential that is not fully reflected in its valuation, with around 32% implied upside to the $10 price target and robust projected total

data.title: Wells Fargo GAAP EPS of $1.60 beats by $0.02, revenue of $21.45B misses by $340M
data.description:
• Wells Fargo press release (WFC): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.60 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $21.45B (+6.5% Y/Y) misses by $340M.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson raises dividend by 3.1% to $1.34
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) declares $1.34/share quarterly dividend, 3.1% increase from prior dividend of $1.30.
  • Forward yield 2.25%
  • Payable June 9; for shareholders of record May 26; ex-div May 26.
  • See JNJ Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: BlackRock in charts: Revenue from investment advisory performance soars Y/Y, securities lending rise
Avanos Medical rockets 67% on $1.27B acquisition by American Industrial Partners
data.description:
• Avanos (AVNS) stock price climbed over 67% on Tuesday during pre-market hours as the medical tech company agreed to be acquired by American Industrial Partners for $1.27B.
  • The deal will be an all-cash transaction where AVNS shareholders will receive $25 per share in cash, implying a ~72% premium to the prior close and ~83% premium to the 30-day average price.
  • The deal has been unanimously approved by the board and is expected to close in H2 2026, subject to shareholder approval and regulatory clearances.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson Non-GAAP EPS of $2.70 beats by $0.02, revenue of $24.06B beats by $450M
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson press release (JNJ): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.70 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $24.06B (+9.9% Y/Y) beats by $450M.
  • Solidifying path to double-digit growth by the end of the decade, with significant progress for patients with the approvals of ICOTYDE the first-and-only targeted oral peptide for plaque psoriasis, TECVAYLI plus DARZALEX FASPRO as early as second line for patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, VARIPULSE Pro in Europe with a new pulse sequence that is now 5 times faster, and TECNIS PureSee Intraocular Lens for U.S. Cataract Patients
  •  
  • Company increases 2026 guidance with estimated reported sales of $100.8 Billion or 7.0% at the midpoint, and adjusted EPS4 of $11.55 or 7.1% at the midpoint
  • FY26 sales consensus of $100.59B, Adjusted operational EPS consensus of $11.55.
  • Shares -1% PM.

data.title: Asgard Therapeutics names Wolfram Brugger as chief medical officer to lead clinical transition
data.description:
• Asgard Therapeutics (AUTL) appointed Wolfram Brugger as Chief Medical Officer, as the company advances its “personalized off-the-shelf” cancer immunotherapy platform toward clinical development.
  • The appointment comes at a pivotal stage for the company as it prepares for clinical entry, with Brugger expected to lead clinical strategy, regulatory engagement, and trial execution.
  • Brugger has been involved in more than 130 Phase I-III clinical oncology trials with numerous modalities across a wide variety of cancer types, including solid tumors.

data.title: Unity Bancorp GAAP EPS of $1.40 beats by $0.02, revenue of $33.61M misses by $0.18M
data.description:
• Unity Bancorp press release (UNTY): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.40 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $33.61M (+14.5% Y/Y) misses by $0.18M.
  • Produced a 2.04% return on average assets and a 16.38% return on average equity.
  • The provision for credit losses on loans was $1.0 million for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, compared to $2.2 million for the quarter ended December 31, 2025.
  • As of March 31, 2026, the allowance for credit losses as a percentage of gross loans was 1.28%.
  • Total deposits increased $55.1 million, or 2.4%, from December 31, 2025.
  • Book value per common share was $35.66 as of March 31, 2026, compared to $34.63 as of December 31, 2025.

data.title: OpenAI's $852B valuation questioned by some investors amid strategy shift: report
data.description:
OpenAI's (OPENAI) $852B valuation is under scrutiny from some of its own backers ​as the company shifts its focus to the enterprise ‌market to compete with Anthropic (ANTHRO), the Financial Times reported.

Several recent deals, initiatives, and abandoned projects are aimed at reorienting the company around a new strategy: defend ChatGPT’s dominance among consumers while taking on Anthropic in the higher-margin market for corporate AI services, the report added.

Some OpenAI investors told the news outlet the changes could leave it vulnerable to Anthropic and a resurgent Google, while preparing for a potential IPO as early as this year, the report noted.

"You have ChatGPT, a 1bn-user business growing 50-100 per cent a year, what are you doing talking about enterprise and code?" said one early backer of OpenAI, according to the report. "It’s a deeply unfocused company."

OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI recently raised $122B in funding, valuing it at $852B, from investors, including SoftBank (SFTBY) (SFTBF), Amazon (AMZN), Nvidia (NVDA), Andreessen Horowitz, Sequoia Capital, and Thrive Capital.

"The suggestion that investors are not supportive of our strategy defies the facts," said OpenAI's CFO Sarah Friar, the report noted. "Our . . . raise, the largest in history, was oversubscribed, completed in record time and backed by a broad set of global investors, reflecting strong conviction in both our direction, current business momentum and long-term value."

The success of Anthropic has led to a strategic rethink. The Claude AI chatbot maker — which is backed by Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google — saw its annualized revenue surge from $9B at the end of 2025 to $30B at the end of March.

Anthropic’s business appears to have surpassed OpenAI, which reached $25B in annualized revenue in February, though the companies use different accounting methods to book revenue, making direct comparison difficult, the report added.

OpenAI’s Chief Revenue Officer Denise Dresser accused Anthropic of overstating their revenue “by roughly $8bn” by “grossing up [revenue] share with Amazon and Google,” in a note to staff on Sunday, as per the report.

Dresser noted that Anthropic’s “coding focus gave them an early wedge” in the race for enterprise customers, as per the report. But, she added that “the market is ours to win."

Friar said the company still had the support of its investors, given that OpenAI closed the biggest private fundraising of all time. The CFO said the $122B raising “gives us a lot of flexibility at this moment in time. I view my job as always [to create] max flexibility, max optionality for the company, because then we can make more strategic decisions.”

One investor who has backed both companies said that to underwrite an investment in OpenAI’s recent round, they would have to assume an IPO valuation of $1.2T or more, the report added.

Data from secondary marketplaces that trade proxies for both companies' stock indicate demand is higher for Anthropic, and, for the first time, buyers are placing a premium on the Claude chatbot maker over OpenAI, the report noted.

Last week, it was reported that OpenAI told investors that its early efforts to increase computing resources gave it a vital advantage over rival Anthropic.
